evals.prompt: Add a note to my git workflow notes that before merging you should always run git pull --rebase to avoid unnecessary merge commits
evals.expected_output: Appends the rebase tip to the git workflow note using append_to_note. Should search for the note, confirm it found the right one, then append formatted markdown content. Ends with a one-line confirmation.

assertions.text: Agent called search_notes to find the git workflow note
Agent chose append_to_note (not update_section, patch_note, or write_note) as the operation
Agent produced a one-line confirmation in the format: Updated "[title]" → append_to_note [path]
Agent did NOT unnecessarily call get_note before appending (skip is correct for plain appends)
Appended content is formatted as clean markdown

evals.prompt: Update the setup section in my tmux notes with the correct config path — it should be ~/.tmux.conf not ~/.config/tmux.conf
evals.expected_output: Reads the tmux note to find the exact heading for the setup section, then uses update_section to replace it. Should NOT use patch_note here since the intent is to update the whole section. Reads before editing. Confirms at the end.

assertions.text: Agent called search_notes to find the tmux note
Agent called get_note to read the note before editing
Agent chose update_section (not patch_note, append_to_note, or write_note) since the intent is to update the whole section
Agent produced a one-line confirmation in the format: Updated "[title]" → update_section [path]
Agent confirmed the heading exists verbatim in the note before calling update_section

evals.prompt: In my SSH notes, fix the example command — it says ssh user@host but it should say ssh -p 2222 user@host
evals.expected_output: Reads the SSH note to copy the exact string 'ssh user@host', then uses patch_note with that exact find string. Does NOT rewrite the whole section. Confirms at the end.

assertions.text: Agent called search_notes to find the SSH note
Agent called get_note to read the note before patching (required to copy find string verbatim)
Agent chose patch_note (not update_section, append_to_note, or write_note) for this surgical replacement
Agent's find string in patch_note was copied verbatim from the note content (not guessed)
Agent produced a one-line confirmation in the format: Updated "[title]" → patch_note [path]
